meta_findings.title: No alternative consumer design considered
meta_findings.description: Pre-mortem identifies P1-PRODUCER-ONLY as critical but never considers WHERE frontmatter_warnings should be consumed. Only mentions 'no hook/router reads this' without evaluating WHICH hook or display mechanism would be appropriate. A proper pre-mortem should have ranked candidate consumers (PostToolUse, StopHook, skill输出, etc.)
meta_findings.evidence: Section 3 'No integration point defined' identifies the gap but Step 5 prevention only says 'Define consumer' without naming candidates
meta_findings.impact: Cannot implement fix without consumer design decision
meta_findings.recommendation: Add explicit consumer ranking: 1) PostToolUse (fires after skill invocation), 2) StopHook (session-end summary), 3) Inline skill output (skill返回时). Rate each by fail-fast alignment.
meta_findings.why_missed: Focus on identifying the problem dominated over solution design

meta_findings.title: T1-IMP path bug severity understated
meta_findings.description: R2 (path mismatch) is rated L=2, I=3 = Score 6, but a 2-year-old path bug that silently defeats validation is a CRITICAL issue. The wrong path means validation 'often not find the file, returning empty warnings even for skills with missing fields'. This is a systematic false-negative that makes the entire advisory system appear to work when it largely doesn't.
meta_findings.evidence: T1-IMP description: 'returns empty warnings even for skills with missing fields — making the system appear to work when it largely didn't'
meta_findings.impact: System provides false confidence; path bug silently discards real warnings for 2 years
meta_findings.recommendation: Re-rate R2 to L=3, I=3 = Score 9, matching R1 criticality
meta_findings.why_missed: Risk scoring treats it as technical bug (L=2) rather than silent data loss (L=3)

meta_findings.title: Success theater detection incomplete
meta_findings.description: Section 3.6 correctly identifies success theater signals (tests pass, function implemented), but misses that the 11 tests themselves are evidence of test-driven producer-side development WITHOUT consumer-side test. The tests verify ledger writes, not user output — this is exactly the 'testing the write' anti-pattern.
meta_findings.evidence: '11 tests pass, but tests only verify the write to ledger, not that warnings appear in user output' — yet R5 (no integration test) is rated only Score 4
meta_findings.impact: Tests provide false confidence that system is working end-to-end
meta_findings.recommendation: R5 should be Score 6+ (L=3, I=3) since integration test absence is what makes R1 latent rather than obvious
meta_findings.why_missed: Success theater section identifies the symptom but doesn't escalate the missing integration test to match

meta_findings.title: No concurrency analysis for multi-terminal scenario
meta_findings.description: Solo-dev environment runs multiple terminals. The ledger write via append_event() has no locking. If two terminals invoke the same skill simultaneously, frontmatter_warnings writes could race. The pre-mortem assumes single-threaded execution.
meta_findings.evidence: System described as 'solo-dev environment' with no concurrency analysis. append_event() to ledger without FileLock or equivalent.
meta_findings.impact: Concurrent skill invocations could corrupt or lose frontmatter_warnings
meta_findings.recommendation: Verify append_event() uses FileLock or session-scoped state. If not, frontmatter_warnings could be lost under concurrent execution.
meta_findings.why_missed: Solo-dev context may have created assumption of non-concurrent execution

meta_findings.description: Of 8 root causes, 4 are categorized as Process (P1-P4) vs 3 Tech (T1-T3) vs 1 External. The pre-mortem focuses heavily on process failures (no consumer defined, no integration point, no definition of done) while potentially under-examining WHY the path bug (T1) persisted for 2 years — which is ALSO a process failure but not labeled as such.
meta_findings.recommendation: T1-IMP (2-year path bug) should be labeled P5-PROCESS to reflect that it persisted due to lack of review, not technical obscurity

meta_findings.description: R1 has L%=95%, C%=95%. The Likelihood % describes probability the risk materializes if unchecked. But R1 HAS already materialized — the system writes warnings that no consumer reads. The probability is 100% that this is happening NOW. The 95% seems to describe 'probability if left unfixed' rather than current state.
meta_findings.evidence: R1 description: 'warnings written but never displayed' — this is current state, not hypothetical
meta_findings.recommendation: Distinguish between 'already occurring' (L=100%) vs 'will occur if unchanged' (future probability). R1-R3 are already happening.

meta_findings.location: R3 risk rating vs T2-LOGIC description

agent_a.name: self-review
agent_a.description: Early-return gate suppresses warnings — rated L=2, I=3

agent_b.name: self-review
agent_b.description: Early-return gate 'suppressing the very warnings the system was designed to capture' — described as critical violation of fail-fast surface

meta_findings.resolution: T2-LOGIC text uses 'CRITICAL' language but R3 rating is only Score 6. R3 should be escalated to L=3, I=3 = Score 9 to match T2-LOGIC's criticality language.

agent_a.name: self-review
agent_a.description: Cleanup changed early-return from 'not skill_file.exists()' to 'not frontmatter'. States this is 'actually CORRECT behavior'

agent_b.name: self-review
agent_b.description: Early-return gate still gates frontmatter_warnings — R3 is still open

meta_findings.resolution: Step 1.5 claims the cleanup 'was a cleanup, not the actual surfacing fix' and R3 'Partial' status承认s early-return still gates. The Step 1.5 language 'actually CORRECT behavior' is misleading — it fixed one bug but R3 remains open. R3 prevention in Step 5 still lists 'When frontmatter_warnings is non-empty, always write state regardless of execution requirements' as TODO.

meta_findings.description: EnforcementTierValidator creates false confidence is rated L=1, I=2, Score=2 — lowest severity. But this finding explains WHY R1 persisted: developers may believe enforcement is covered by EnforcementTierValidator, reducing urgency to fix advisory system. This secondary confusion factor directly sustains the primary failure mode.
meta_findings.recommendation: E1-EXT should be rated higher (Score 4-6) as it is a force multiplier for R1 — without E1-EXT creating false confidence, developers might have fixed R1 earlier

meta_findings.description: Path mismatch is identified as a 2-year-old bug but only L=80%, C=70%. The fact it persisted 2 years suggests it's more like L=95% (high persistence = hard to detect). However, the '70%' confidence in 'will cause failure' may be accurate if it describes 'when it causes visible failure' rather than 'when it silently fails'.
meta_findings.recommendation: Clarify C% definition — if it means 'confidence this will cause visible failure', 70% is reasonable; if it means 'confidence this is a real bug', should be 95%

meta_findings.description: No integration test for consumer path rated L=80%, C=90%, Score=4. But 11 tests exist and none verify consumer path — this is 100% certainty the integration test is missing. L=80% understates certainty. The C=90% is also questionable since there's explicit grep evidence that no consumer exists.
meta_findings.recommendation: R5 should be L=100%, C=95% — the missing integration test is a certainty, not a probability

summary.consensus_issues: N/A - single analyst self-review, no cross-agent consensus to measure
summary.blind_spots_summary: 4 blind spots identified: missing consumer design alternatives, understated R2 severity, incomplete success theater analysis, no concurrency analysis
summary.bias_summary: 2 bias patterns: process over-focus (4 P-category vs 3 T-category), confidence inflation on R1 (already materialized, not future risk)
summary.contradictions_summary: 2 contradictions: R3 severity vs T2-LOGIC criticality language; Step 1.5 'correct' vs R3 still open
summary.calibration_summary: 3 calibration issues: E1-EXT under-rated, T1-IMP ambiguous confidence definition, R5 underconfident on missing integration test certainty
